chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Time When Breaking the Fast Becomes Permissible and Prayer Becomes Obligatory

text: 1932 - رَوَى عَمْرُو بْنُ شِمْرٍ عَنْ جَابِرٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : «إِذَا غَابَ اَلْقُرْصُ أَفْطَرَ اَلصَّائِمُ وَ دَخَلَ وَقْتُ اَلصَّلاَةِ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.1932 - Amru ibn Shimr narrated from Jabir, from Abu Ja'far (as), who said that the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said:
"When the disk of the sun disappears, the fasting person may break their fast, and the time for prayer begins."
